reason: STEP 3 DIRECTION NOT EXPLICIT: Step 3 goes from integral_R p^a(t)(dg^b/dt)(t) phi(t,gamma_bar(t)) dt to integral_R dt integral_{R^3} d^3x p^a(t)(dg^b/dt)(t) delta^3(x-gamma_bar(t)) phi(t,x). The transition uses the sifting property of delta^3 in the REVERSE direction: phi(t,gamma_bar(t)) = integral delta^3(x-gamma_bar(t)) phi(t,x) d^3x. The proof does not explicitly state which direction the sifting property is being applied. While the identity is symmetric (both sides are equal), stating the direction would improve clarity. Also, the interchange of the dt integral with the d^3x integral (going from iterated to double integral) implicitly uses Fubini's theorem, which is justified since the integrand (with the delta distribution) has compact support in t for each fixed test function phi. This Fubini application is not mentioned.
